pending.body: finding_summary: The new bead-export path deserializes `br show --json` into `BeadDetail`, but the real `br` CLI returns a one-element array. `materialize_beads()` does this on every create/reuse path (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1784-1802`, `2014-2023`), while `BrCommand::show()` always emits `br show <id> --json` (`src/adapters/br_process.rs:352-355`) and `exec_json()` blindly parses into the requested type (`src/adapters/br_process.rs:769-779`). I verified locally in this repo that `br show ralph-burning-9ni.4.6 --json | jq -r 'type'` returns `array`. As written, `milestone export-beads` will raise `BrParseError` the first time it tries to resolve a created bead or inspect an existing one. The new tests miss this because the helpers at `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:5235-5300` mock `show` as an object rather than the real array payload.

finding_summary: The idempotency/reuse logic only treats `open` and `in_progress` beads as reusable, and converts any closed/deferred milestone-scoped title match into a hard failure. `summary_matches_materialized_bead_except_role()` filters matches to `Open | InProgress` (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1904-1908`), then `find_existing_materialized_bead()` returns an error when no eligible match remains (`1968-1981`) or when a valid match coexists with a non-active historical duplicate (`1984-2007`). In practice, rerunning `milestone export-beads` after some previously exported beads have been completed or deferred will fail instead of skipping those existing beads and exporting newly added work, which breaks the command's expected idempotent/incremental behavior.

status_summary: running: final review restart round 4 -> Plan and Implement
